data_coverage_summary: Samsara exposes broad fleet, equipment, industrial, route, maintenance, safety, compliance, and sensor APIs, including snapshot, history, and feed-style endpoints for locations, stats, readings, and event subscriptions.
buyer_guidance: Use Samsara when you need a telematics-heavy API that combines vehicle, equipment, trailer, industrial asset, maintenance, and route data with feed endpoints for operational telemetry and webhook-based event delivery.
overview: Samsara's developer portal documents APIs for vehicles, equipment, trailers, gateways, routes, drivers, hours of service, maintenance work orders, safety events, industrial assets, and sensor-style data inputs. The public reference also lists snapshot, history, and feed endpoints for vehicle stats, vehicle locations, equipment stats, equipment locations, data inputs, route events, and webhook subscriptions, plus write-capable endpoints for assets, routes, work orders, and industrial asset data outputs. That makes Samsara a strong source for both asset master data and operational telemetry. Asset data is Supported. Telemetry data is Supported. Writeback is Supported.

available_data.category: Fleet, equipment, and industrial asset records
available_data.description: The documented surface covers core operational entities plus related maintenance and assignment records.
data_points: Vehicle, trailer, equipment, gateway, and asset records
Industrial asset records and data outputs
Route, hub, order, and plan entities
Maintenance work orders and DVIR defects

available_data.description: Samsara publishes both current-state snapshots and continuous or incremental feeds for operational telemetry.
data_points: Vehicle location history, feeds, and snapshots
Vehicle stats history, feeds, and snapshots
Equipment locations and stats history, feeds, and snapshots
Readings definitions, snapshots, history, and feed endpoints
Sensor door, humidity, temperature, cargo, and history data
Route events, alerts, safety events, faults, trips, and idling events
relevant_operations: Follow a feed of equipment stats

ingest_considerations: Samsara distinguishes between snapshot, history, and feed endpoints, so ingestion design should explicitly separate current-state syncs from streaming or incremental telemetry collection.

The surface is write-capable across assets, routes, work orders, webhooks, and industrial data outputs, so production integrations should isolate read-only and write-capable credentials where possible.

No exact public machine-readable API description was confirmed during this review, so the developer portal reference remains the evidence anchor until Samsara exposes a direct downloadable spec artifact.

source_evidence.evidence_notes: The public Samsara developer portal lists fleet, equipment, industrial, route, safety, maintenance, webhook, and readings endpoints, including feed, history, and snapshot variants for locations, stats, and sensor-style data.
